finding: A mycotic aneurysm of the aortic root and sinus of Valsalva may be associated with infectious

differentials.name: PRIMARY MYCOTIC ANEURYSM (rare) unassociated with any demonstrable intravascular inflammatory process
differentials.raw: PRIMARY MYCOTIC ANEURYSM (rare) unassociated with any demonstrable intravascular inflammatory process

differentials.name: SECONDARY MYCOTIC ANEURYSM =  aneurysm due to nonsyphilitic infection
differentials.raw: SECONDARY MYCOTIC ANEURYSM =  aneurysm due to nonsyphilitic infection

differentials.name: Aortic trauma ← accidents, aortic valve surgery, arterial graft, coronary artery bypass surgery, intravascular catheter, arterial catheterization, joint prosthesis
differentials.raw: Aortic trauma ← accidents, aortic valve surgery, arterial graft, coronary artery bypass surgery, intravascular catheter, arterial catheterization, joint prosthesis

differentials.name: Immunocompromise
differentials.raw: Immunocompromise (malignancy, alcoholism, corticosteroid therapy, chemotherapy, autoimmune disease, diabetes)

differentials.name: persistent fever, leukocytosis ← bacteremia (positive blood culture in only 53%); NO acute chest pain
differentials.raw: persistent fever, leukocytosis ← bacteremia (positive blood culture in only 53%); NO acute chest pain

context: endocarditis, uni- / bicuspid aortic valve, and infected prosthetic aortic valve! Predisposing factors: Risk factors: Mechanism: (a)  hematogenous: ›  septicemia with abscess formation via vasa vasorum ›  septicemia with abscess formation via vessel lumen (b)  direct extension from adjacent infection: osteomyelitis of sternum or spine, renal or psoas abscess → weakening + destruction part of the aortic wall (c)  traumatic / iatrogenic: intima laceration (trauma, atherosclerosis, coarctation) Organism:  S. aureus (53%) > E. coli > Salmonella (33-50%) > nonhemolytic Streptococcus > Listeria > Ha
